in_scope: Board CRUD — read/write .excalidraw files and .meta.json sidecars
Skill runner: regenerate (local), sync and analyze (via Pacer -> claude CLI)
Neo4j graph read (search, label, text queries) and write (via skills)
Filesystem watcher (chokidar) broadcasting board/snapshot change events over WS
Per-project Neo4j Bolt connection resolution from config.yaml
properties.name: NaCl Analyst Tool
properties.description: Local web application (Fastify + React/Excalidraw) that lets BA/SA practitioners browse, edit, and round-trip .excalidraw board files with the underlying Neo4j graph via deterministic renderers and whitelisted NaCl skill invocations. Replaces legacy excalidraw + excalidraw-room Docker containers.
out_of_scope: Public Excalidraw cloud (excalidraw.com) — no dependency or integration
Containerised / hosted deployment — tool runs only on analyst machine
GitHub Actions CI/CD for this tool itself
Direct hand-written Excalidraw JSON in production paths
Any claude -p subprocess spawned outside itsalt-pinch Pacer
constraints: Must not be containerised — shells out to local claude binary
All graph mutations route through deterministic renderers
Project registry is single source of truth for project root + active project
Graph infrastructure is per-project (each project has its own graph-infra/ and Neo4j container)
assumptions: claude CLI installed and logged in on PATH
One active project at a time; project switch reloads Neo4j driver
goals: Provide a single deterministic interface for inspecting and editing Excalidraw boards
Round-trip board state with Neo4j graph via renderers (regenerate) and skill sync
Invoke NaCl skills (sync, analyze) via itsalt-pinch Pacer against local claude CLI
Maintain project registry and per-project graph connections
Ship as an npm CLI (nacl-analyst-tool) installable on the analyst machine
